digest.component_or_area: op-cert trusted gate service and client command
propositions: A trusted service fetches authoritative ticket and code state itself so client-supplied inputs cannot forge a passing gate cert.
An async job API enqueues gate runs (202 job_id) and a bounded serial background worker drains them to a terminal status.
Requests accept only ticket_id and kind identifiers; extra client fields like material_fingerprint or commit are ignored and the server derives all bound values.
Per-kind dispatch maps plan-review to PASS/BLOCK/INDETERMINATE and completion-verifier to PASS/FAIL, emitting the DSSE envelope only on PASS with asymmetric error handling per gate.
The signing key is materialized from SSM to a 0600 mkstemp file for ssh-keygen and deleted in a finally, with REBAR_OPCERT_KEY_PATH overriding the seam key resolution.
The server runs gates in an ephemeral workspace with push disabled so no tickets-branch event is written; the client persists the returned envelope as a SIGNATURE event.
